results.title: Rex: AI Operations Partner for Enterprise Order to Cash
results.content:
Rex is an AI-native service for order to cash. We replace people-heavy operations with AI agents that continuously manage invoices, customers and exceptions. Order to cash is a $60B outsourced function because the work is too messy for traditional automation. Every customer has different context, exceptions do not follow clean rules, and the next step is often buried in emails, portals, handoffs, or someone’s memory. Rex agents run continuously in the background and resolve work to SLA standards so cash keeps moving. We're live with pre-IPO tech companies, managing >$500m in receivables from F100 companies, cutting manual follow-up cycles from weeks to hours. We founded Rex after spending four years building Sequence, an a16z-backed finance ops platform, from zero to Series A — where we [...] Merlin Kafka

Hey Product Hunt, I'm Merlin, co-founder of Rex. Lewis and I spent four years building AR software at Sequence. What we kept seeing: someone on the finance team copying data from a portal into a spreadsheet into NetSuite, every single day. Automation tools never fixed it because the work is full of exceptions and judgment calls. So we built Rex to just do the work. Agents plug into your existing stack and run collections, portal uploads, and the AR inbox end to end. Anything sensitive gets routed to your team. Synthesia runs their AR on us today. Ask me anything, including what the agents still get wrong.

results.title: AI in order-to-cash: automating the full revenue cycle | Rex
results.content:
## How Rex runs order-to-cash

Rex is an agentic AI accounts receivable agent. It works collections, cash application, and disputes across your whole ledger, continuously, taking the actions a team would otherwise take by hand. It chases the invoice through the right channel, applies the payment when it lands, opens and works the dispute, and stops to ask a person only when a case needs a human decision. It is measured on cash recovered and DSO down, not on dashboards rendered. [...] # AI in order-to-cash: automating the full revenue cycle

AI agents are starting to run the order-to-cash cycle end to end, not just dashboard it. Here is where they fit across O2C and why collections is the place to start.

AI in order-to-cash: automating the full revenue cycle

AI in order-to-cash means an agent does the work across the revenue cycle, from credit and invoicing through collections and cash application, rather than a person driving each step by hand. The agent reads the state of every account, decides the next action, takes it across your systems, and escalates only what needs a human call. That is different from a dashboard that shows you the work, and different from RPA that runs a fixed script. [...] Building toward an autonomous O2C is a phased path, not a switch you flip. Connect the systems so agents can read and act. Set policies and approval thresholds. Start an agent supervised on collections, where the outcome is clearest, and let it earn authority as it proves itself. Then extend the same pattern to cash application, then disputes, then upstream into credit and billing. Each stage rests on the trust the last one built, which is why AR first is not a limitation but the right sequence.

## How Rex runs order-to-cash

results.title: Transform the order-to-cash process with AI agents
results.content:
AI agents offer a better alternative. They provide the scalability of outsourcing without the cost and inefficiency of human labor. They can work 24/7, handle both inbound and outbound workflows simultaneously, and ensure every communication feels contextual and human-like. Instead of paying a shared service provider to hire more clerks, companies can deploy AI agents that grow with the business, improve cash flow, and reduce Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) without expanding headcount.

## Conclusion

AI agents are redefining the order-to-cash process. They can interpret contracts, create invoices, manage finance inbox queries, automate dunning and collections workflows, resolve disputes, and streamline cash application and reconciliation. [...] AI agents are reshaping order-to-cash (O2C) by taking on the repetitive, communication-heavy work that credit controllers and AR analysts have always handled by hand. Plenty of O2C leaders have reached for rules-based automation and RPA, only to watch it fall apart the moment a workflow hits unstructured data, an exception, or a customer conversation.

AI agents reach further. They handle mailboxes across billing queries and collections. They handle edge cases in cash application, resolving disputes and deductions. Agents manage your supplier portals and perform self-billing reconciliations. They make credit approvals and monitor risks. AI agents do the actual O2C work. [...] Product

# Transform the order-to-cash process with AI agents

## Executive summary

AI agents are redefining the order-to-cash process. They can interpret contracts, create invoices, manage finance inbox queries, automate dunning and collections workflows, resolve disputes, and streamline cash application and reconciliation.
Where shared services reduce cost by moving manual work offshore, AI eliminates the manual work altogether. The result is faster collections, fewer disputes, stronger cash flow, and finance teams that can focus on strategy instead of repetition.
